claim: The Lancet AMR study estimated 1.27 million deaths directly attributable to AMR and 4.95 million associated deaths in 2019, with SSA and South Asia carrying the highest burden and lowest surveillance capacity.

source.title: Global burden of bacterial antimicrobial resistance in 2019: a systematic analysis

method: Reviewed systematic analysis methodology, burden estimates, and regional comparisons.
limitations: Burden estimates rely on modeling where surveillance data is absent.
Surveillance-capacity estimates are inferred from reporting, not directly measured.

claim: WHO GLASS 2023 reports AMR surveillance from 127 countries but fewer than 40 report sub-national data and fewer than 20 meet quality standards for bloodstream-infection surveillance.

source.title: Global Antimicrobial Resistance and Use Surveillance System (GLASS) Report 2023

method: Reviewed GLASS report enrollment, data quality, and regional coverage.
limitations: GLASS enrollment does not guarantee data quality or completeness.
Sub-national reporting is voluntary and incomplete.
